prompt:
Question: Which of the following passages is the original verbatim passage? The other passages are paraphrased versions of it.

Options:
A. The 2014 Australian federal budget was the first time the Abbott government delivered a budget for the 2014/15 financial year since the 2013 election. It was presented to the House of Representatives by Treasurer Joe Hockey on May 13, 2014, to address a perceived deficit crisis. This included a proposed significant reduction in government bureaucracy, significant changes to welfare, new initiatives for a medical research fund, and spending on roads. The budget was not expected to exceed 1% of GDP until 2023, and the Australian public expressed strong disapproval of the austere budget.
B. The 2014 Australian federal budget outlined the financial plan for supporting government services and operations throughout the 2014/15 fiscal year. This 2014 budget marked the initial one presented by the Abbott government, following the Coalition's triumph in the 2013 Australian federal election. Treasurer Joe Hockey formally submitted the budget to the House of Representatives on May 13, 2014.

The budget incorporated substantial revisions aimed at tackling a perceived shortfall crisis. These revisions encompassed a proposed substantial reduction in government administrative bodies, alongside considerable alterations to welfare provisions, novel programs for a medical research fund, and investments in road infrastructure. A budgetary surplus surpassing 1% of the Gross Domestic Product was not anticipated to materialize before 2023. This stringent budget encountered extensive disapproval and was largely rebuffed by the Australian populace, a sentiment clearly indicated by all public opinion surveys conducted subsequent to its unveiling. C. The 2014 Australian federal budget was the federal budget to fund government services and operations for the 2014/15 financial year. The 2014 budget was the first delivered by the Abbott government, since the Coalition's victory in the 2013 Australian federal election. Treasurer Joe Hockey presented the budget to the House of Representatives on 13 May 2014.The budget featured significant changes to address a perceived deficit crisis. This included a proposed dramatic downsizing of government bureaucracy, and contained significant changes to welfare, new initiatives for a medical research fund and spending on roads. A budget surplus exceeding 1% of GDP was not expected until 2023.The austere budget faced widespread criticism and was overwhelmingly rejected by the Australian public as reflected in all opinion polls after its release.
